reasoning: The ECB kept policy rates unchanged at its March 19, 2026 meeting, maintaining stability in the broader interest rate environment. The recent start of the new Reserve Maintenance Period (around March 25) saw the €STR ease slightly to exactly 1.930, down from the 1.932–1.934 range observed earlier in the month. Since the rate on March 27 is 1.930 (a local minimum for March), mean reversion strongly suggests that normal daily fluctuations (typically 0.001 to 0.002) are more likely to push it slightly upward than downward. Furthermore, while the approaching quarter-end (March 31) can temporarily suppress rates due to banks' balance sheet window-dressing, early April (April 1-3) usually sees a 'bounce-back' as banks resume normal unsecured overnight borrowing, adding upward pressure. Combined with the FRED tool's baseline statistical probability of 0.67, these factors make a minor increase (to 1.931 or higher) the most likely outcome, though I adjust slightly downward to 0.61 to account for the possibility of the rate pegging exactly at 1.930 or drifting marginally lower.
